constraints.description: Presence of common HTML/XML characters like '<' or '>' in text fields suggests accidental scraping/parsing errors (noise).
constraints.expression: any('<' in str(row[c]) or '>' in str(row[c]) for c in ['title', 'category', 'condition', 'spec_brand', 'spec_model', 'spec_type', 'spec_style'])

constraints.description: Using a common placeholder like 'N/A', 'unknown', or an empty string in a structured field suggests an accidental data entry or scraping error (noise).
A non-empty spec_upc that is not a 12-digit string is an invalid format, suggesting an accidental data error (noise).
A non-empty spec_ean that is not a 13-digit string is an invalid format, suggesting an accidental data error (noise).
A negative or implausibly large (> 20000) description_length is physically nonsensical, indicating an accidental data error (noise).
A title shorter than 5 or longer than 80 characters (eBay's limit) is implausible for a real listing, indicating an accidental data error (noise).
A return_period_days value other than one of the standard options {7, 14, 30, 60} when returns are accepted is invalid, indicating an accidental data error (noise).
A negative seller_feedback_score is impossible on eBay, indicating an accidental data or parsing error (noise).
Accepting returns but not specifying a return period is usually an accidental form submission error, not a deliberate omission (noise).
Specifying a return period while not accepting returns is contradictory and usually an accidental error from using a template (noise).
A non-positive price is impossible for a listing, indicating an accidental data error (noise).
A seller_feedback_pct outside the valid [0, 100] range is impossible, indicating an accidental data error (noise).
A negative n_images count is physically impossible, indicating an accidental data error (noise).
A returns_accepted value other than 'True' or 'False' is invalid, indicating an accidental data error (noise).
